title: acaena pumila
intro: Acaena pumila, also known as dwarf or New Zealand thyme, is a low-growing, spreading groundcover plant native to New Zealand. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it useful for treating wounds, skin issues, and respiratory problems. The health benefits of Acaena pumila include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and boost the immune system. Acaena pumila can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and topical creams, making it a versatile and useful plant for natural health remedies.
